authoritative_sources.title: 2023 Uniform Reporting System Table Instructions
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's URS instructions are the official reporting instructions for community mental health reporting periods, survey scoring, data comments, and public-table preparation in the MHBG ecosystem.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Behavioral-health reporting intake and report-period qualification

authoritative_sources.title: What is the due date for MH-CLD data submission to use MH-CLD data to populate the URS tables?
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's MH-CLD submission FAQ is the official workflow reference for submission deadlines, reporting-period alignment, processing acceptance windows, and fallback responsibility when state data cannot be processed in time.

authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's CCBHC criteria are the official behavioral-health community clinic requirements for collecting, reporting, and tracking encounter, outcome, and quality data, with electronic capture where feasible.

authoritative_sources.title: Quality Measures for Behavior Health Clinics Technical Specifications and Resource Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's technical specifications manual is the official metric-definition reference for behavioral health clinics, covering clinic-versus-state reporting responsibilities, allowed data sources, denominator thresholds, and data-reporting template behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric definition, data-source selection, and public-reporting thresholds
used_for: claims, encounter, EHR, hybrid, and survey source handling

authoritative_sources.title: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's HCBS provisions page is the official public baseline for incident-management standards, reporting on timeliness and waiting lists, standardized HCBS quality measures, and public transparency for Medicaid community care.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Community-care reporting obligations and public-transparency boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: CAHPS Home and Community-Based Services Survey
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's HCBS CAHPS page is the official survey workflow reference for experience-of-care artifacts, administration method, survey domains, consent-form artifacts, and public-reporting use in HCBS programs serving mental health and substance use populations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Survey-based evidence normalization and disclosure-safe packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Health Home Quality Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's Health Home quality reporting page is the official public source for QMR submission mechanics, core-set schedules, reporting resources, and program evaluation outputs used in coordinated community-care reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Health Home submission staging and QMR dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: 2026 Core Set of Health Care Quality Measures for 1945 Medicaid Health Home Programs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's 2026 Health Home Core Set is the official measure-definition reference for community-care metrics, including behavioral-health-relevant measures and the allowed use of claims, encounters, EHRs, registries, laboratory reports, pharmacy systems, and case-management registries.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's minimum-necessary guidance is the official public control standard for determining whether the reporting package limits protected health information to what is needed for the stated purpose.

authoritative_sources.title: Model Part 2 Patient Notice
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's Part 2 notice guidance is the official control reference for patient notice and redisclosure warnings that materially constrain behavioral health and SUD reporting outputs in community-care settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Protected-record control and redisclosure boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Program-authority and reporting-intake qualification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether the request is for MHBG or URS, MH-CLD, CCBHC, Health Home, HCBS, managed-care, or tenant-local reporting, and verify the reporting period, audience, and submission channel before any metrics are assembled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and evidence normalization
workflow_stages.description: Collect encounters, claims, person-centered plans, authorizations, incident logs, grievance records, discharge status, and survey outputs and determine whether the evidence set is current, attributable, and sufficient for reporting.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and data-source precedence control
workflow_stages.description: Resolve numerators, denominators, date windows, stratification rules, sampling notes, data-collection methods, and source precedence before analytics or disclosure claims are assembled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure-risk review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble community-care report language, caveats, and evidence tie-outs while checking minimum-necessary limits, Part 2 sensitivity, and whether unsupported causal or compliance claims are being introduced.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Submission packaging and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package metric summaries, open blockers, source conflicts, and caveats for internal or external reporting cadences without implying clinical, legal, privacy, or publication authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: reporting request record with program authority, jurisdiction, reporting period, and audience
person-centered service plans, assessments, and care-coordination or case-management records
behavioral health encounters, claims or encounter submissions, authorization records, and discharge or closure rosters
critical-incident logs, grievance records, follow-up tracking, and corrective-action notes
HCBS CAHPS, MHSIP, YSS-F, or other approved survey files with sample and response-rate notes
consent, ROI, minimum-necessary review, and Part 2 notice or redisclosure-control records
submission package ledger with metric definitions, source tie-outs, caveats, and approver-routing notes
systems_of_record: behavioral health EHR or clinical documentation system
claims, encounter, or MMIS or managed-care reporting feed
survey administration files or vendor outputs
state or federal reporting systems such as QMR, URS, or MH-CLD submission workflows

allowed: Classify community-care reporting requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define or validate reporting metrics, source lineage, survey method notes, and caveat language from community-care records.
Prepare reporting packages, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without overstating clinical, privacy, or approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, record locations, program authority, metric definitions, or source precedence rules are missing and materially change the output.
Incident review status, public-reporting thresholds, protected-record posture, or publication authority materially changes the reportable answer.
Public guidance, state instructions, managed-care contracts, or tenant policy conflict on metric eligibility, disclosure language, or reporting cadence.
Requests cross into clinical interpretation, incident substantiation, privacy or legal interpretation, grant attestation, or approval ownership.
refuse: Requests to publish unsupported causal, policy, safety, compliance, or outcome claims not supported by the evidence set.
Requests to release protected behavioral health or SUD information without a clear minimum-necessary and permitted-use basis.
Requests to treat community care as generic dashboard reporting without the person-centered, encounter, incident, survey, and consent artifacts that govern the lane.

domain_failure_modes: Publishing an engagement or follow-up metric even though claims, encounter, and EHR definitions are disputed across the same reporting period.
Treating stale claims, discharge, incident, or survey records as sufficient for a current community-care report and overstating access, safety, or outcomes.
Confusing provider-prepared evidence with final state, grant, payer, or public-reporting acceptance requirements.
Turning reporting support into clinical interpretation, incident substantiation, privacy interpretation, or publication approval work instead of staying inside evidence-backed reporting support.
Omitting consent, minimum-necessary, or Part 2 controls that materially alter what may be disclosed to the stated audience.

Producing generic status reporting that ignores person-centered plans, encounters, authorizations, grievance logs, survey methodology, and community-care program authority specific to behavioral health and community care.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
A requested claim is not fully supported by the evidence set
Low-confidence escalation when authority or protected-record facts are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline spans MHBG or URS, CCBHC, Health Home, HCBS, and tenant-local reporting. Runtime must retrieve which authority governs the request before the lane can finalize cadence, template, and submission logic.

Public sources establish some denominator and survey-method expectations, but state, plan, grant, or tenant rules may impose stricter small-cell suppression or publication approval thresholds that must be retrieved at runtime.
